title: Qualify the deterministic simulator product journey
outcome: The complete central loop and applicable adverse states pass deterministic simulator automation and the native evidence matrix on an immutable candidate revision; simulator-insufficient claims remain for WP-DEVICE-01.

prohibitedPaths.reason: program package lanes consume but do not mutate the plan

interfaceContracts: Fixtures represent verified contracts and never grant authority.
Every simulator case names runtime/device/config/revision and privacy-safe evidence.
Simulator evidence never claims signed-device behavior.

splitIf: more than 20 files or 3 primary roots are required
a second high-contention lock is required

fallbacks: GitHub connector or gh read-only evidence

required: remaining blockers and next safe action
handoff.splitOrReassignWhen: After one bounded status request, interrupt only if no material evidence; reassign untouched disjoint work, never a second writer on the same paths.

backend.dependency: Record source, environment, deployed revision, and evidence type separately; a merge is not deployment.

git.prTitle: WP-JOURNEY-01: Qualify the deterministic simulator product journey

mergePredicate: all acceptance criteria and invariants pass on exact head
fresh required local validation passes on exact head
independent final-diff review has no unresolved P0/P1/P2
all applicable required GitHub checks succeed
PR is conflict-free and branch protection/review requirements hold
base/head drift is reconciled and invalidated gates rerun
backend deployment and owner/device gates are truthful
PR is not #117 and frozen branch is untouched
cleanupPredicate: GitHub records merge and target contains reviewed change
worktree has no live PID/open file
worktree is clean with no unmerged commit
remove with git worktree remove, never rm -rf
delete only merged package-owned local/remote branch when not a stack dependency
reverify target ancestry, worktrees, and primary checkout fingerprint
releaseBoundary: Development source only. No deployment, App Store, TestFlight, signing/release action, PR #117 mutation, or release-readiness claim.
